Teaching

Philosophy

I believe in an active learning approach where theoretical concepts are reinforced through hands-on experimentation and simulation. My goal is to equip students not just with knowledge, but with the critical thinking skills required to solve real-world engineering problems.

ME 301: Thermodynamics I

Fall 2023, Fall 2024

Fundamental concepts of thermodynamics, first and second laws, properties of pure substances, and entropy.

Undergraduate

ME 450: Fluid Mechanics

Spring 2024

Fluid statics, control volume analysis, Navier-Stokes equations, dimensional analysis, and viscous flow.

Undergraduate

ME 620: Advanced CFD

Spring 2023

Numerical methods for solving fluid flow equations, turbulence modeling, and grid generation techniques.

Graduate

Dr. Robert Anderson

Department of Mechanical Engineering, Tech University

Email: r.anderson@university.edu | Office: Engineering Hall 304
